programmes.show_name: Stacey Dooley: Face to Face with Armageddon
programmes.show_description: The journalist meets Americans prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest.
When travelling musician and drug user Carmen goes into multiple seizures in the jail's intake area, officers call a 'Code Blue' emergency as staff and paramedics scramble to respond.
Hard-working ambulance crews help a shivering pensioner with possible sepsis, as well as a sparky 95-year-old who's had a fall.
On shift with West Midlands Ambulance Service who treat a young lad with a fever, a golf-loving pensioner with a heart problem and a woman convinced she has lung cancer.
Shopping from Home.

programmes.show_name: Gemma and Gorka: Life Behind the Lens
programmes.show_description: Gemma and Mia spend some time with furry friends as Gorka gets ready for a new dance show. Gemma's gym sessions reach the halfway point, and she makes a splash at the BRITS.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items in Wensleydale, North Yorkshire, that are ready for upcycling and hopefully turn a profit for their owners.
Basildon: Inspirational property makeovers. Nick and the team head to Basildon to transform the home of nine-year-old Brandon who has severe epilepsy.
Swansea: Nick Knowles and the team are in Swansea to transform the home of fifteen-year-old Jack Morris who was born with a rare form of muscular dystrophy.

Another couple seek help from the Scott twins in the property renovation series. Janna and Jason want a large urban dream home where they can raise a family.
Popular property renovation series. Can Drew and Jonathan Scott help Patrick and Julie find the pedestrian-friendly property they want?
Orpington: Inspirational property makeovers. The team are off to Orpington to transform Jason Sulong's home after a motorcycle accident changed his life forever.
Basildon: Inspirational property makeovers. Nick and the team head to Basildon to transform the home of nine-year-old Brandon who has severe epilepsy.
Dudley crews attend a man with heart failure and sepsis, as well as a transgender patient who's in a lot of pain from an old injury. In Stoke, a patient has a panic attack in the ambulance.
One more shift with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. The team are called into action when an explosion demolishes a block of flats.
The response team race to the rescue of a worker trapped in a collapsed trench, and paramedic Kim must risk her own life to give him medical treatment 10ft underground.
Eye-opening two-part series with the ever buoyant Louis Theroux. Louis does time in an ultra-violent Miami prison where cellmates live by a vicious gladiatorial code.
Louis risks life and limb in a monster prison in Miami chock full of deadly criminals. In the concluding part, he cosies up to an alleged triple murderer.
An asthmatic deteriorates rapidly in the ambulance, and the crews are called to a 30-year-old car that's been driven into a wall by a 90-year-old driver.
The West Midlands crews treat a pensioner whose heart rate is so erratic they are worried he might have a heart attack on the way to the hospital.
Paramedics blue light it to a man who had a bad fall and has a suspected spinal injury, while Ash and Craig help a 12-year-old boy who has been attacked at his local fun fair.
Shopping from Home.
Little Girl Lost: Part 2: The baby's body is found and the evidence stacks up against the nanny's boyfriend, but Sam and Jacob's gentle interrogation produces another startling suspect.
Teenage Kicks: Part 1: When a talented young athlete is found battered to death, officers interrogate guests at a house party he had attended earlier that evening.
Teenage Kicks: Part 2: Heaton plays a psychological game, putting pressure on the teenage partygoers by holding a press conference to uncover the truth about the death of their friend.
Feet of Clay: Part 1: Human remains found in a park turn out to be those of a student who went missing 20 years previously, and the case brings Heaton face-to-face with an old adversary.
Feet of Clay: Part 2: The discovery of two more bodies convinces Heaton his suspect is a serial killer, but the man he sent to prison 20 years ago sticks to his story that he is innocent.

Reality series following the work of Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. There is a suspected acid attack on the streets of Bradford.
Another shift with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. A police chase ends with a spectacular crash and five trapped teens in the wreckage.
More real-life drama with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. The team race to save an injured woman trapped in a van crushed between two lorries.
Hit the road with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. This episode features a stand-off involving a man with a samurai sword in South Yorkshire.
Amy deals with a serious emergency during a birth, Caroline must rapidly help her patient breathe again, and Rhona treats Keith, who may have a life-threatening infection.
Three young newly-qualified twenty-something nurses Renee, Caitlin and Military Nurse James cope with the pressures of working for the NHS and finding a work-life balance.
Louis Theroux heads to America to investigate one of the most controversial and captivating icons of recent times: gun-toting, self-described 'gay hillbilly' and 'Tiger King' Joe Exotic.
